_meta.source_note: Seeded from NVD, CISA KEV, and vendor advisories. CVE counts and MTTP are representative figures based on public record through mid-2025. Validate against live NVD API before publishing.

vendors.name: CrowdStrike
vendors.wr_take: Best patch velocity in endpoint. The July 2024 outage was an ops failure, not a security failure — an important distinction the market conflated.

cve_list.description: Falcon sensor local privilege escalation via kernel driver race condition
Falcon console stored XSS via malformed alert description field
Falcon Data Replicator S3 path traversal allowing read-only access to adjacent tenant data
SentinelOne agent local privilege escalation on Windows via unquoted service path
Management console SSRF via webhook configuration endpoint
PAN-OS authentication bypass in management web interface — exploited in the wild pre-patch
GlobalProtect gateway arbitrary code execution via malformed handshake packet
Panorama SQL injection via device group filter parameter

vendors.name: Fortinet
vendors.wr_take: FortiOS has been a persistent KEV fixture for three years running. At some point this stops being bad luck and starts being architecture.

cve_list.description: FortiOS SSL-VPN out-of-bounds write allowing unauthenticated RCE — actively exploited by multiple threat actors
FortiOS format string vulnerability in fgfmd daemon enabling unauthenticated RCE
FortiAP stack-based buffer overflow in CLI command handler

vendors.name: Ivanti
vendors.wr_take: Ivanti's remediation pace is structural, not situational. 16 active KEVs is not bad luck — it reflects product lifecycle debt that marketing cannot patch.

cve_list.description: Ivanti Connect Secure stack-based buffer overflow — zero-day exploited by UNC5337 (PRC-nexus) before disclosure
Ivanti Connect Secure local privilege escalation — chained with CVE-2025-0282 in active campaigns
Ivanti EPMM command injection via API — required emergency repatch after incomplete initial fix
Windows Update rollback vulnerability enabling reintroduction of patched flaws — exploited ITW
Windows Hyper-V elevation of privilege — zero-day exploited before Patch Tuesday disclosure
Windows DWM Core Library privilege escalation — used in QakBot post-exploitation chain
Okta Classic Engine session token fixation allowing account takeover without MFA
Okta on-prem AD agent authentication bypass — legacy agent versions only
Cisco NX-OS CLI command injection allowing root code execution — exploited by Velvet Ant (PRC)
Cisco ASA and FTD management tunnel DoS — part of ArcaneDoor campaign
Cisco ASA persistent backdoor mechanism via crafted VPN client package
Splunk Enterprise path traversal on Windows enabling unauthenticated arbitrary file read
Splunk Enterprise RCE via insecure session storage deserialization — requires auth

vendors.name: Broadcom / VMware
vendors.wr_take: Post-acquisition patching velocity has degraded noticeably. vCenter and ESXi remain tier-1 targets for ransomware operators. The Broadcom integration has not helped.

cve_list.description: VMware vCenter Server heap overflow in DCERPC protocol enabling unauthenticated RCE
VMware vCenter Server privilege escalation to root via DCERPC — chained with CVE-2024-38812
VMware Workstation and Fusion XHCI USB controller use-after-free enabling VM escape
Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway unauthenticated RCE — mass exploitation in 2023/2024 by multiple ransomware groups
Citrix Virtual Apps and Desktops privilege escalation via session recording component
Schneider EcoStruxure Panel Server unauthenticated path traversal allowing config file exfiltration
Modicon M340 PLC hardcoded credentials in web server component

vendors.name: SolarWinds
vendors.wr_take: Meaningful improvement since SUNBURST. Patch cadence is faster, disclosure is more transparent. Still carries reputational weight that procurement teams haven't fully priced in.

cve_list.description: SolarWinds Web Help Desk Java deserialization RCE — unauthenticated, exploited ITW
SolarWinds Web Help Desk hardcoded credential allowing unauthorized DB access
Check Point Security Gateway information disclosure — path traversal enabling credential extraction, exploited ITW
Check Point Quantum Security Gateway local privilege escalation via kernel module
Tenable Nessus path traversal allowing authenticated read of arbitrary server files
